TextENGNew: ``Huh?@k@vS06/02/150200190. Mii-chan, you look like you didn't get much sleep either.@k@vS06/02/150200191. Don't you?``
``...Yeah.@k@vS06/06/150601983. I didn't go to bed until about three.@k@vS06/06/150601984. ...I'm sleepy.``
``Th-Three?!@k@vS06/01/150100176_1. Come on now... there's a limit to how late you should stay up!``
``You probably got hooked on a manga series and ended up reading every volume from the start, didn't you?@k@vS06/01/150100178. Yeah yeah, I've been there myself.``
``......Huh... could it be... about the mayor?@k@vS06/02/150200194. He still hasn't been found?``

changeReason: Old translates うん as 'Nope' which is the opposite of 'Yeah'; a direct meaning reversal.

TextENGNew: After that, I'd gone to Furude Shrine to meet up with the youth group, and we split up to search everywhere.
Since I alone knew he would absolutely never be found, the whole affair was unbearably tedious.
At school, the teacher explained that the mayor had gone missing and asked if anyone knew anything, requesting a show of hands.
One or two students raised their hands and answered, but their responses were so completely off the mark that I nearly laughed.
The mayor's disappearance, which to me was nothing more than an itch on my back, remained the hot topic among my classmates well into lunchtime...
For the time being, I observed Keiichi Maebara.
Just watching him like this, there was no way to tell whether any danger was closing in around him.
...Tonight, I would talk to him again, calling as Shion.
Last night I'd hit him with too much shocking information at once, so he'd panicked considerably.
But surely after a night's sleep, he would have regained his composure.
Just then, a girl's laughter reached my ears--some kind of playful exchange with a classmate.
It was Rika Furude.@k ...The last survivor of the Furude family, and its current head.
That said, her treatment as head was minimal at best.
At the clan councils, she'd crawl into Grandmother's futon to nap or sit there drawing pictures.
...At yesterday's board meeting, she hadn't even been invited.
It was hard to believe that such a child, in her capacity as head, bore any part of the curse system as one of the Three Families...
But even if she wasn't expected to carry the dignity of a family head...
...there were far too many old people who fanatically worshipped her as the reincarnation of Oyashiro-sama.
...Rika Furude herself was rather peculiar, and there were those who said she seemed to act with an awareness of being Oyashiro-sama's reincarnation... where had I heard that?
...Perhaps it was something Takano-san told me at the library, back when she was still alive.
When Rika Furude expressed concern about something, the fanatical elders would take action.
...That structure was no different from the Sonozaki main family's chain of command, and no different from Hinamizawa's curse system.
......Did Rika Furude know anything about the deaths of Takano-san and the others?
What about the break-in at the ritual storehouse?@k Had she been told who entered, and what had become of them...?
...As a member of the Furude family, which managed the ritual storehouse, there was a high chance she was the first to detect the intrusion.
In fact, she might have been the only one who could detect it. Perhaps the curse was carried out precisely because she reported it...?
changeReason: Old states 'She must have passed on that information' as fact; new correctly preserves the speculative tone (のではないのか) with 'Perhaps the curse was carried out because she reported it.'

TextENGNew: When we entered the ritual storehouse, that fool Keiichi flipped the light switch.
It wasn't far-fetched to think that switch was connected to some mechanism--an intrusion alarm of sorts.
...Rika Furude, head of the Furude family.@k She was well worth making contact with.
``...As head of the Furude family, how much do you know about this incident?``
My question was blunt and direct.
Whether Rika understood that I was speaking not as Mion Sonozaki but as the acting Sonozaki family head...@k was difficult to read from her inscrutable expression.
``...Are you asking about Tomitake and the others?``
But Rika didn't play dumb--she readily mentioned Tomitake-san by name.
changeReason: Old simplifies to 'Rika answered,' omitting the key observation that she 'didn't play dumb and readily mentioned Tomitake-san by name' (変にとぼけたりせずに富竹さんの名前を口にして見せた).

TextENGNew: She clearly knew about the incidents of that night, which shouldn't have been public knowledge.@k Despite her childish appearance, her title as Furude family head was apparently no mere formality.
``...All I can say is that it was Oyashiro-sama's curse.``
changeReason: Old omits Rika's qualifier としか言えないのです ('that's all I can say'), which conveys that she is limited in what she can reveal--an important narrative detail.

TextENGNew: ``I already know that.``
``Then it's not over yet, is it?@k@vS06/06/150601988. Two remain.@k@vS06/06/150601989. Have you heard what will happen to them?``
I deliberately kept things vague here, wanting to observe how Rika Furude would respond.
I had been assuming that since she was treated as a child, she had nothing to do with Hinamizawa's dark underbelly--but that assumption might have been based solely on appearances.
After all, Rika had just casually spoken about the night's incidents, which ordinary people shouldn't have known about at all.
...That was proof she was part of the underside's information network.
``.........I don't think it really matters, you know.``
Given how unhesitatingly she'd mentioned Tomitake-san and the others, this response from Rika came after a suspiciously long pause.
Had she needed a moment to think about her answer?
...Damn, this girl's face never changes.@k I can't read her thoughts at all.
``'Doesn't matter'? That can't be right.@k@vS06/06/150601991. You understand that they can't just get off without atonement, don't you?``
changeReason: Old renders けじめ (atonement/accountability) as 'How can they get away with it?' (general outrage); new correctly frames it as a pointed question about atonement to Rika.

TextENGNew: ``...I think if they properly reflect on what they've done, that should be enough.``
Having witnessed the elders' fury at yesterday's board meeting, this was an utterly unexpected response.
Violating the ritual storehouse was an act of blasphemy against Oyashiro-sama.

While those old men had raged that such offenders deserved death,@k the head of the Furude family--guardian of that very sacred storehouse--was saying that reflection was sufficient. An extraordinarily lenient position.
...I was reminded of how Rika Furude's father, the shrine priest, had been regarded during the dam conflict.
That's right.
...If I recall correctly, her father had been called a fence-sitter during the dam conflict.
He had voiced objections to the Guardians' radical resistance movement against the dam construction.
...Like father, like daughter, I suppose.
I aggressively grabbed Rika Furude by the collar and lifted her up.
``Hah?@k@vS06/06/150601993. What kind of wishy-washy nonsense is that?@k@vS06/06/150601994. The Furude family, from the previous head to you--you're all hopelessly soft, aren't you?``
changeReason: Old translates 甘ったるい (soft/lenient/indulgent) as 'stupid'; new correctly conveys 'unbearably soft,' reflecting a criticism of the Furude family's leniency, not their intelligence.

TextENGNew: ``Your Furude family, of all people, should understand best how sacred and inviolable the ritual storehouse is.@k@vS06/06/150601996. How can the head of that family say something so lenient?``
I laughed at myself.
The very same words that had been used against me, making me tremble at my own sin--and here I was turning them around to threaten someone else.
changeReason: Old simplifies to 'I was told the same thing and was now saying it to Rika'; new preserves the crucial self-awareness detail that the narrator 'trembled at her own sin' (自分の罪に慄いていた) when those words were used against her.

TextENGNew: ``Do you not understand how grave a crime it is to violate the ritual storehouse?@k@vS06/06/150601998. Don't pretend you don't!``
``...The ritual storehouse is a very important place for Oyashiro-sama.@k@vS06/05/150500034. ...It's a place no one should enter without permission.``
``See, you do understand.@k@vS06/06/150602000. Those four brought defilement into that sacred place.``
``...Bad kitty cats, they are.@k@vS06/05/150500036. ...Meow.``
``That's right, bad cats.@k@vS06/06/150602002. Two have already been caught and punished.@k@vS06/06/150602003. Two more remain.``
``How should they be made to atone?@k@vS06/06/150602005. I'd like to hear the opinion of the Furude family head.``
Rika truly was the type whose expression almost never changed, making her thoughts nearly impossible to read.
But as I held her by the collar and stared her down, I gradually began to pick up traces of expression.
...Rika was noticeably unsettled, and rather than answering my question, she seemed to be trying to probe my true intentions.
``...I want to forgive... those two kitty cats.``
``You think that qualifies as atonement?!``
``...Mii... I don't really understand what 'atonement' means.``

``Good question--let me educate you.@k@vS06/06/150602008. Atonement means paying for your sins.@k@vS06/06/150602009. Sins cannot be left unaddressed.@k@vS06/06/150602010. Because that would mean leaving Oyashiro-sama's anger unresolved.``
``Sins must be cleansed through atonement proportional to their severity.@k@vS06/06/150602012. You understand that, right?``
``...Oyashiro-sama doesn't actually get angry even if someone sees inside the storehouse.``
``Huh? What are you talking about?``

``...The reason no one's allowed to see inside is because there are scary tools in there.@k@vS06/05/150500042. I think it would frighten people if they saw them.@k@vS06/05/150500043. That's why we keep it off-limits...``
``I'm sure the people who went in were scared too.@k@vS06/05/150500045. So I think they've already learned their lesson.``
``Learned their lesson, and that's enough to be forgiven?!@k@vS06/06/150602015. That's not for you to decide--that's Oyashiro-sama's judgment!!``
``...But I'm Oyashiro-sama's shrine maiden, you know?``
``Of course I know that!!@k@vS06/06/150602017. Are you telling me you've personally heard Oyashiro-sama say he's not angry?!``
Rika nodded yes without the slightest hesitation.
Seeing that, I was astounded at how shamelessly she could spout such fabrications.
No--it went beyond astonishment. I was simply dumbfounded.

``Ha!!@k@vS06/06/150602019. This is exactly why your parents died in the curse two years ago.@k@vS06/06/150602020. Oyashiro-sama struck them down because they were unfit to serve him!!@k@vS06/06/150602021. That's why your father was cursed to death!!``
``...Oyashiro-sama would never do such a thing.@k@vS06/05/150500048. ...Father didn't die because of Oyashiro-sama's curse...``

``Good grief!!@k@vS06/06/150602023. And this is the last shrine maiden of the Furude family!@k@vS06/06/150602024. No wonder Oyashiro-sama can't find peace!!@k@vS06/06/150602025. You're supposed to be the one who enshrines Oyashiro-sama!@k@vS06/06/150602026. How pathetic.``
``...I don't really understand why you're so angry, Mii...``
I became certain of something.
Setting aside her actual influence, this girl was in a position to know as much about the village's dark underbelly as the other Two Families' heads.
The proof was in how openly and casually she discussed this year's incidents.
...And yet she could play dumb with a straight face like this.
...What a terrifying child!@k In that regard, she was several levels above me!
I slapped her face two, three times, then shoved her roughly by the collar.
changeReason: Old says 'pushed her to the floor' (not in JPN); new correctly translates 胸倉を乱暴に押してやった as 'shoved her roughly by the collar.'

TextENGNew: Rika's light body tumbled and rolled from that alone, and she looked up at me with eyes brimming with tears.
``This whole thing started because you wanted to change the storehouse's lock since the heavy one was too much trouble, right?``
``...That's true.@k@vS06/05/150500051. ...The old one was too stiff for me, so... I asked Kimiyoshi to replace it with an easier one.``
``If you hadn't been too lazy to deal with a proper lock, the intrusion might never have happened.@k@vS06/06/150602029. You bear part of the responsibility for this whole affair, you know?``
``Don't forget that.@k@vS06/06/150602031. Of course, the Kimiyoshi family head who helped you swap the lock shares responsibility too...``
That last remark was somewhat off the cuff.
I simply incorporated Rika's mention of consulting Kimiyoshi about the lock to connect it to Kimiyoshi's disappearance.
I wanted to apply psychological pressure on Rika by doing so.
``The Kimiyoshi family head... has been made to atone.@k@vS06/06/150602033. That's why he vanished.``
``The Furude family head will also be expected to atone...``
Overwhelmed by the sudden malice directed at her, Rika could only spill tears from her glistening eyes, lying where she'd been pushed, sprawled on the ground.
``The only one left is Keiichi Maebara.@k@vS06/06/150602036. ...I'll leave him to the Furude family to handle.``
``...I... don't know anything...``

``You don't need to know anything, do you?@k@vS06/06/150602038. You just need to look troubled, to show concern. That's all it takes.@k@vS06/06/150602039. It's the same at our house.@k@vS06/06/150602040. ...Anyway, I'm counting on you.``
``...That's not...``
Rika hung her head.
She looked like she wanted to say something back, but seemed unable to speak for fear that she'd be roughed up again.
...Whether Rika Furude truly occupied a command position in the curse system like Grandmother was somewhat doubtful.
But there was no question that as one of the Three Families, she was positioned far closer to the dark underbelly than I was.
...With this, the bait that was Keiichi Maebara should have thoroughly permeated the underside of Hinamizawa.
At yesterday's board meeting, I'd already announced before the village's leading elders that Keiichi Maebara was the remaining target.
And now I had told the head of the Furude family as well.@k Threateningly, at that.
If this threat was effective, the 'Curse of Oyashiro-sama' should descend on Keiichi Maebara in the very near future.
I would hook whoever came for him at just the right moment... and drag my enemy into the open.
If the assassin came from Rika Furude's line, this girl would bear the same guilt as Grandmother.
...When that time came, I would show no mercy, child or not.
I would make her suffer terribly before killing her...
...That's right, I'd heard of a torture where dozens of nails are hammered into the victim's fingers.
I'd already found the restraining table and full set of implements for it last night.
...When the time comes, I'll fill every one of those innocent little fingers with nails...
That cruel anticipation flowed from my eyes as undisguised malice, making Rika shrink back even further.
Speaking of which, how was Kimiyoshi doing in the secret room?
His share of responsibility for constructing and spreading the curse system of Oyashiro-sama was now clear.
His sins were of the highest order.
I'd asked him nearly everything I wanted to last night, and now that I knew he didn't know anything beyond that, there wasn't much point deliberating further.
...Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan... I really did like him.
He'd always been kind to me.
Because Grandmother was especially harsh with her own family,@k ...he made up for it by being extra gentle, spoiling me.
When we talked yesterday...
...I was so happy when he said he would forgive Shion.
When he said he would fight Grandmother to save her, I was truly happy.
Don't worry.@k If Shion-chan truly regrets it, there's no way she'll be demoned away.@k Leave it to Ojii-chan...
My chest grew warm.@k ...Trembling... quivering.@k ...Happy... and yet, sad.
Those words from Ojii-chan that made me tremble with joy...@k I never dreamed they would lead directly into him saying Satoshi-kun deserved to die.
Read in context, that conversation should have been about cheering up Mion, who was worried about Shion's fate.
A cursed Houjou brat getting killed was only natural, but Shion-chan of the Sonozaki family wouldn't be killed.@k That was the contrast he'd been drawing.
In other words, to the village elite like Kimiyoshi, the Houjous were less than vermin.
Whether they lived or died... not a ripple of emotion.
The sin of Kimiyoshi and his ilk was openly displaying their contempt for the Houjou family and letting it infect the entire village.
Because of that, everyone in the village considered it natural for them to fall victim to Oyashiro-sama's curse.
...Under the name of Oyashiro-sama's curse... killing them was no problem at all.@k ...They had created that very environment.
If that was the case...
...If this year's victims, Takano-san and the others, hadn't committed the supreme transgression of violating the ritual storehouse...
...This year's curse might have targeted the last survivors of the Houjou family--Satoko Houjou and Teppei Houjou, who was apparently hiding somewhere in Okinomiya.
...Satoko Houjou.
Always clinging to Satoshi-kun, depending on him for everything.
She believed that if she cried and clung to him, Satoshi-kun would fix everything.@k ......She was one of the people who drove him into a corner.
If Satoko hadn't been such a burden on Satoshi-kun...@k ...he never would have been driven to murder his aunt.
...Someone instigated Satoshi-kun to kill his aunt,@k and to erase any trace of contact with that mastermind, Satoshi-kun himself was 'demoned away'...@k That was Ooishi's hypothesis, if I recall.
If that was the case, had Satoshi-kun never crossed the dangerous bridge of killing his aunt,@k perhaps he wouldn't have been chosen as last year's victim...?
After all, the only order that had been issued was the vague directive that the curse of Oyashiro-sama would befall the Houjou family.
Yes--any member of the Houjou family would have sufficed as a sacrifice.
The wicked aunt apparently had a terrible reputation in the neighborhood.
She was awful with the neighbors, there was absolutely nothing to respect about her--a hysterical hag.
...Yes, even if Satoshi-kun hadn't gone so far as to kill her, someone else would surely have carried out the curse on her.
So the aunt dies from the curse. That's one.
Then, to appease the curse, another Houjou as sacrifice.
The uncle was rumored to be living somewhere in Okinomiya.
But Okinomiya, as a territory, was somewhat harder to control than Hinamizawa.
The Sonozaki main family certainly wielded strong influence there as well,@k but it wasn't a place where one could brazenly carry out a 'demoning away' in broad daylight.
Then naturally, Satoshi-kun and Satoko, who might as well have been living right in the village's belly, would have been the chosen targets.
Between Satoshi-kun and Satoko... which one would the curse fall upon?
That was obvious.
Satoko, of course. That cheeky, ill-mannered little brat.
Satoshi-kun was calm and well-liked in the neighborhood.
...Even as a survivor of the Houjou family, the greatest traitors of the dam conflict,@k ...he was far less deserving of death than Satoko, at least.
In that case... the true intended victims of last year's curse should have been... the aunt and Satoko.
Satoshi-kun would have been left all alone overnight.
But... no more cruel aunt. No more cruel uncle either.
...And Satoko, who had essentially been a parasite on Satoshi-kun simply by virtue of being born his younger sister, would also be gone.
Satoshi-kun was such a kind person. He would surely grieve even as he realized he was finally free.
But... time heals wounds... and I could heal him too.
Then Satoshi-kun wouldn't need to force himself to keep living in that Hinamizawa house.
My hideaway apartment building had plenty of other empty rooms.
For part-time work to support himself, I could rely on relatives who were close to me.
He wouldn't need to commute all the way to the school in Hinamizawa anymore.@k He could just go to school with me in Okinomiya.
Satoshi-kun had probably never attended school in Okinomiya, and wouldn't know his way around the area very well.
So I would always be by his side, showing him the way.@k ...Shortcuts to school, back alleys, hidden little shops worth visiting... I could teach him all sorts of things.
It would be... an unbelievably happy ending.

For Satoshi-kun, who had been forced into such a painful life, and for me, forever the one cast into the shadows...@k A truly modest but warm happy ending that might have been waiting for us within this gloomy story.
I couldn't afford to linger even in fantasies that were only permitted in hazy daydreams.
Apparently, many of the elders who had been calling all day without reaching Grandmother planned to try again around the time I'd be home from school.
...The phone had been ringing at regular intervals, preventing me from indulging in my fantasies with Satoshi-kun.
I responded to the information and consultations brought by the old people with vague agreement or casual deflection.
Just as I'd planned, word had already spread throughout the underside of Hinamizawa that the next curse would fall on Keiichi Maebara.
Tying it to the mayor's disappearance, I also leaked the dubious information that his vanishing was atonement for being the one responsible for swapping the storehouse's lock...
The underside's elite trembled at the gravity of violating the ritual storehouse--and the terrifying weight of a situation where even a Three Families head was being punished for it.
Among the incoming calls was one from Ooishi.
Ooishi, too, had already caught wind of the rumor that Kimiyoshi had been made to take responsibility in connection with the lock replacement.
...Impressive.
This man's intelligence network was the real deal.
But ultimately, all this man could do was cup his hands and catch whatever information leaked out.@k He could never reach the source of those leaks, or what swirled and lurked deeper still.
Later tonight, I would call Keiichi Maebara again.
By now, the more aggressive types must have begun prowling around Keiichi.
Even Keiichi must be starting to feel the danger closing in on him, sensing it in his very skin.
I needed to be careful that Keiichi didn't get killed too easily.
If I could reel in the enemy before they bit Keiichi, I could use the same bait for another round of fishing.
...Keiichi Maebara.
My connection with him was recent and brief.
Not the brightest, but amusing in various ways.
...Of course, he could never come close to replacing Satoshi-kun,@k but he was entertaining enough to make you think it'd be fun having someone like him around.
There was exactly one thing about him that I found unpleasant.
...The way he casually stroked my head.
And the fact that his hand had exactly the same warmth as Satoshi-kun's... that was unforgivable.
But that was all.
...Keiichi Maebara wasn't Satoshi-kun's enemy, and I bore him no grudge.
I found him fun to be around, but if he fell victim to the curse, it wouldn't weigh too heavily on my heart.@k That was the extent of it.
...I would let Keiichi Maebara die, but I wouldn't actively kill him myself.
I had maneuvered things so the curse would target him, but I never told anyone to kill him.
...Though I suppose today, I did explicitly tell Rika Furude that the Furude family should deal with Keiichi Maebara.
...Wait a minute.
To avenge Satoshi-kun's regret,@k I was doing the exact same thing as the grandmother I despised most,@k and using the very curse system that was Satoshi-kun's enemy.
...Keiichi Maebara.
I was merely using him.
...I had incited the curse against him, but I would give him enough advice to defend himself.
I would support Keiichi's struggle to survive.
If... I managed to catch all my enemies using Keiichi as bait...@k ...then perhaps I could release him.
He was different from Satoshi-kun, who had been instigated into killing his aunt and then 'disappeared' to erase evidence of contact with the mastermind.
changeReason: Old says Satoshi was 'killed himself afterwards'; new correctly translates 消された as 'disappeared' (made to vanish), preserving the ambiguity of Satoshi's fate that is central to the story.

TextENGNew: I would not make Keiichi Maebara disappear.
In that one respect alone, I intended to be different from Grandmother and her ilk...
changeReason: Old uses singular 'the hag'; new correctly translates 祖母たち (grandmother and others) as plural 'Grandmother and her ilk.'

TextENGNew: Outside, the evening sky was giving way to night.
I needed to prepare dinner.@k ...I had to eat and keep up my strength--otherwise I couldn't carry out my plan of revenge.
This was the countryside, far from town, and in winter the heavy snow could seal the village off entirely.
So having two or three large refrigerators was perfectly normal here.
Inside them was more than enough food to sustain me for quite a while.@k ...Not having to go shopping was a tremendous relief.
Through the window, the chorus of higurashi cicadas crept in.
Many people say the song of the higurashi evokes a sense of nostalgia.@k ...But for me, it was the opposite.
I'd only ever heard higurashi in Hinamizawa.
So to me, the higurashi's song was the sound of Hinamizawa,@k ...a melody that only reminded me I didn't belong here, that I wasn't supposed to be here... filling me with loneliness.
Just then... the doorbell rang.
The Sonozaki main house had such a long approach that a doorbell and intercom were installed at the front gate.
There was even a security camera.
Having that surveillance monitor sitting in the quaint old country kitchen looked deeply incongruous.
I flipped the monitor's switch but the screen wouldn't come on.
...Thinking the plug might be disconnected, I tried to check the wiring behind it, but it was a tangled mess running behind the refrigerator, and I couldn't make sense of it.
While I was trying to figure out whether it was broken or I was operating it wrong, the doorbell rang several more times.
...Ideally, I wanted to see who it was before deciding whether to answer or pretend no one was home.
Pretending to be out would have been the easiest option, but it would serve no purpose.
I was out here baring my fangs, hunting for enemies.
I was watching for the enemy from the shadows, vigilant and ready--that was entirely different from cowering in hiding.
Grandmother was supposedly bedridden with illness and foul temper.
I'd already told various people that she wouldn't receive visitors.
...So who would still come calling despite that?
...I steeled myself and turned on the intercom.
If it was a troublesome visitor, I could simply turn them away at the gate.
But I needed to interact as naturally as possible, or I might inadvertently arouse suspicion...@k Act natural, act natural...
``Hello.``
She didn't give her name, but I recognized her instantly from that distinctive way of speaking.
``Oh, is that you, Rika-chan?@k@vS06/06/150602044. ...What's the matter?@k@vS06/06/150602045. At this hour?``
``...Actually, I came to get some soy sauce.``
Soy sauce??@k I barely suppressed a bewildered exclamation and thought about the situation.
In Hinamizawa, where the town was far away and people often stocked up in bulk, it wasn't unusual to occasionally run out of seasonings and go to a neighbor's house to borrow some.
After all, in winter the heavy snow could bury the village and make it impossible to reach town.
However, Furude Shrine and the Sonozaki main house were not close to each other.
Coming all this way by bicycle just for soy sauce was slightly unnatural.
...Was 'getting soy sauce' just a pretext... and she actually wanted to discuss something privately?
Just then, I noticed a piece of mimeograph paper attached to the refrigerator door next to the intercom with a magnet.
'We still have plenty of authentic homemade soy sauce. Please feel free to stop by the Sonozaki house.'
It appeared to be a notice Grandmother had circulated through the neighborhood association.
A distant relative had sent a barrel of soy sauce, and there was more than enough.@k Bring a container and help yourself... that was the gist.
I see--so coming all this way for soy sauce wasn't suspicious after all.
There was a handwritten note at the bottom of the mimeograph, apparently from one of the household helpers.
'Soy sauce is under the floor by the dry goods shelf.@k Give them as much as they want, OK.'
The floor in front of the shelf had under-floor storage.
When I opened it, sure enough, there sat a squat barrel of soy sauce, with a funnel and ladle stored alongside it.
...I didn't want anyone thinking something was amiss at the Sonozaki main house. Not yet.
I decided to share some soy sauce with Rika.
``Sure, that's fine.@k@vS06/06/150602047. Did you bring something to put it in?``
``I brought a soy sauce bottle.``
``Okay.@k@vS06/06/150602049. Let yourself in.@k@vS06/06/150602050. I'll be waiting at the front door.``
As I headed for the entrance, the doorbell rang again.
``...Mii, the gate's locked from the inside. I can't get in.``
``Oh, right.@k@vS06/06/150602052. Sorry, sorry.@k@vS06/06/150602053. I'll come open it--hang on.``
Hinamizawa being a rural area with little concern for crime prevention, most households were rather lax about locking up.
...Having grown up in a somewhat rougher town, locking doors was second nature to me.
I had to put on sandals and go outside just to let Rika Furude in.
``...Good evening.``
``...Good evening.``
Rika smiled brightly--'nipah'--while clutching a large glass bottle.
It was as though nothing had happened earlier today--as if I'd never yelled at her, handled her roughly, or made her cry.
Or perhaps she'd come to curry favor, thinking she had angered me.
I guided Rika to the entrance, told her to wait there, and tried to take the bottle from her.
But Rika wouldn't hand over the bottle, and instead took off her shoes and came inside.
``It's fine, I'll fill it for you. Just wait here.``
``...I've gotten pretty good at pouring soy sauce with a funnel, you know...?``
She looked up at me with eyes that were somehow both pleading and coquettish.
She wanted to fill the bottle herself, apparently.
...I didn't particularly want to let her further into the house, but@k on reflection, leaving Rika alone here while I went to the kitchen was also somewhat unsettling.
It might actually be safer to bring her along to the kitchen where I could keep an eye on her and make sure she didn't try anything.
I told her to do it herself then, and led Rika toward the kitchen.
We walked down the hallway, our footsteps echoing softly.
...Despite two of us walking, I could only hear my own footsteps.
Growing anxious about whether Rika was really following me, I stopped abruptly.
Apparently startled by my sudden stop, Rika took one extra step and bumped softly into my back.
``Mm... ah, sorry about that.``
Confirmed that she was right behind me, I resumed leading the way.
...Tap, tap, tap.
Pat, pat, pat.
...Tap... tap.@k ...Pat... pat.
This girl was apparently amusing herself by matching her footsteps perfectly to mine.
Proof of this: even when I abruptly broke my walking rhythm, our footsteps never fell out of sync--they immediately overlapped again.
Tap tap, tap tap.
Pat pat, pat pat.
Tap tap tap.@k ...I stopped suddenly.
Pat pat pat.@k Pat.@k Rika halted too, one step late.
When I turned around, Rika seemed to realize she'd been caught at her game and smiled mischievously.
Our footsteps were always in unison, and whenever I stopped unexpectedly, there was always that one extra step.
...Those footsteps--I had already experienced them close at hand, and... for some reason, they left me feeling uneasy.
Though in this girl's case, while the footsteps sounded the same, when I turned around, she was actually there.
In that respect alone, these footsteps were different from the ones I knew...
In the kitchen, the under-floor storage from before was still open, the soy sauce barrel peeking out.
The memo said to give as much as they wanted, but I wondered if there was still enough.
...I crouched down and tried to open the lid of the soy sauce barrel.
Even in that simple motion of crouching, from behind me came that one extra soft footstep--pat.
``Yeah, there's still plenty.@k@vS06/06/150602058. Here, take as much as you like.``
When I turned around, Rika was unbelievably close--right in my face.
But... eh?@k ...Who?@k Rika was there, but... a different Rika. One that looked just like her but somehow wasn't.
It was like when my sister and I switched places and the disconnect showed.
...Identical on the outside, but revealing a different personality within.
Did Rika... have a twin?
That confused thought lasted only an instant.
A small spray bottle that Rika was holding--something like a cosmetics spritzer--was aimed at my eyes and fired.
Searing pain tore through both eyes, and tears and mucus poured out like a waterfall as my body desperately tried to flush the agony away.
The stinging sensation made me sneeze uncontrollably.
For several seconds before I recognized I was under a deliberate attack, I was defenseless--covering my face, rolling on the ground.
I squeezed my eyelids shut with all my might, and when I forced them open through the tears,@k Rika had seized a fistful of my hair and was trying to drag me to the floor.
The moment Rika saw our eyes meet and realized I'd recovered my vision, she mercilessly sprayed me again.

I managed to shut my eyes in time, but the chemical apparently entered through my nasal membranes.@k While I avoided the direct searing pain, the terrible tears, runny nose, sneezing, and pain blinded me all over again.
...Ugh, ow ow ow ow...!!
How could my confused brain be this slow...!
Only after all of this did I finally comprehend that I had been attacked by Rika Furude.
But once I grasped that, my mind kicked into higher gear than ever...!!
Rika Furude had rolled me onto my back and was straddling me.
I thrashed around the kitchen trying to escape,@|@y@k crashing into the stove,@|@y smashing into some kind of jar or pot,@|@y toppling a pile of something as I fought desperately.
But Rika wouldn't let me go, clinging tenaciously,@k until she was on top of me again,@k pinning me down.
She pressed down on my stomach without mercy, making me groan from the pain and pressure.
She fought with a practiced ferocity that belied her appearance...!
With the pain in my eyes, I remained robbed of sight.
Pinned down and unable to resist, I was in genuine mortal danger.
Through the narrowest gap between blinks, I glimpsed the face of Rika pressing down on me.
Granted, I didn't know Rika Furude all that well.
My contact with her was limited to the handful of times I'd attended school as Mion, plus whatever information I'd been given beforehand.
But... from those scant encounters, imagining this side of Rika Furude would have been impossible.
Impossible, impossible, impossible!
...No, stop--don't fill your head with the word 'impossible'!
Believe you can handle the situation calmly, don't surrender your mind to confusion, stay cool, stay cool!!
Damn it! That kind of chaotic thinking IS the confusion!!
I peeked at Rika once more through a sliver of blinking.
In one hand was the pepper spray.@k But what was in her other hand was extraordinary.
I doubted my eyes, but what else could I possibly mistake it for?
Its shape was unmistakable.@k Yes, it was... a syringe.
When Rika noticed I had regained my sight,@k she thrust the pepper spray right at my face and blasted me mercilessly.
I clamped my eyes shut and struggled to escape even a fraction of the pain!
My eyes were squeezed shut at that moment, so I couldn't see what happened next.@k But an unforeseen miscalculation befell Rika.
...Using pepper spray at such close quarters in a grapple meant the spray reached her too.
Hearing Rika sneeze repeatedly in choking fits, I knew my greatest chance had arrived!
I didn't need to see--I knew she was right there.
I punched Rika in the face several times,@|@y@k and the instant I felt her weight wobble, I broke free from her hold.
I quickly put distance between us and raised my stun gun.
It seemed Rika had only inhaled a small amount of the spray, because her sneezing had already subsided and she was able to fix me with a malicious glare.
``...Oh, oh, oh... so you wanna pick a fight, do you?@k@vS06/06/150602062. You want me to tear you apart?!!!``
Rika maintained her defiant expression, but there was no doubt she felt the considerable setback of failing to achieve her objective with the initial ambush.
Rika flinched slightly at my roar,@k but still held the pepper spray up, aiming for my eyes.
Her plan was to blind me with the spray and render me helpless,@k then use that opening to... inject me with whatever was in that syringe.
What drug filled it I couldn't tell from the seemingly transparent liquid.
But whatever the substance, its effects were surely terrifying.
Blind me, pin me, force the drug into me wherever she could.
...If the drug was slow-acting,@k I could continue to resist afterward,@k and might very well land a fatal counterattack on Rika Furude.
But Rika's ambush plan seemed to operate on the assumption that simply administering the injection would be sufficient.
...In other words, whatever its effect,@k the drug was fast-acting, taking effect immediately upon injection,@k and its effect would render me unable to fight back.
That meant it was either instantaneous unconsciousness or death--one of the two...!
Well then--my weapon was equally decisive.
My stun gun was a powerful weapon.@k A lethal weapon that could drop someone with a single hit.
And its reliable, devastating power had been proven in every use up to this point.
Rika, too, appeared to understand that my stun gun was a far more powerful weapon than her pepper spray, and she didn't dare make any careless moves.
``...Hey now, how long are you going to stand there gawking after barging into someone's house?@k@vS06/06/150602064. If you won't come to me, I'll come to you!!!``
I grabbed a bundle of newspapers at my feet and hurled it at her with all my strength.
The bundle had only been stacked, not tied, so it burst open right in Rika's face and engulfed her.
My charge came at the exact same moment I threw the newspapers.
I lunged in the instant of Rika's opening.@k Stun gun!!!
With a sharp crack, Rika's body jerked and crumpled to the floor.

...You utter fool!!@k Hesitating when both sides can end it with a single blow!!@k Only amateurs stare each other down like that.@k You clearly have no idea how overwhelming the advantage of striking first is in a one-hit-kill scenario!!!
The spray bottle rolled away.@k But she still had the syringe clutched in her hand!
I'd landed the stun gun, but I wasn't about to let my guard down.
I kicked the crumpled Rika in the side, then straddled her--the positions now reversed.
I took the syringe from her easily.
I stomped on Rika's arm to pin it, then plunged the stolen syringe into that same arm.
changeReason: Old says 'held Rika's arm down'; new correctly translates 腕を踏みつける as 'stomped on Rika's arm,' depicting significantly more violence.

TextENGNew: There was none of the delicacy you'd expect from a vaccination.
I jabbed it in with enough violence to shatter bone.
I pushed the plunger down, forcing every last drop of the drug in roughly.
In response, perhaps, Rika's body began to twitch and convulse, jerking spasmodically.
I backed away from Rika and watched to see what changes would come over her body.
I kept my guard up, of course.
Even though I'd guessed it was a fast-acting drug that would immediately strip away resistance, I needed to maintain distance since I didn't know exactly what reaction it would produce.
``...Ha... ha!@k@vS06/06/150602066. Serves you right!!@k@vS06/06/150602067. Did you actually think you could beat me?@k@vS06/06/150602068. Don't you dare underestimate me!!!``
After trembling and convulsing for a while, Rika unsteadily pulled herself to her feet, bracing against the wall.
...Despite her frail-looking body, she'd taken a direct hit from the stun gun and still managed to stand...
...I checked the output dial on my stun gun.
Ah, damn it!@k The power was on its lowest setting!
It must have gotten bumped during our scuffle...
I cranked the dial to maximum output.
Rika's face had gone deathly pale, cold sweat beading across her entire body.
...I didn't know what the drug's effects were, but had Rika's ambush succeeded as planned, those symptoms would be mine right now.
She looked this bad on the outside, so it wasn't hard to imagine how much worse she felt inside.
Unfocused eyes, swaying body, hand braced against the wall.
...She must have lost her sense of balance--simply standing had to be agonizing.
The defiance that had been in her expression moments ago was gone now.
She simply swayed, occasionally letting out strange cries as if fighting nausea, groaning as though about to retch.
``...Ooh... hah, haah... ahooh... hanyuu... hanyuuu... gah... cough! Cough!!``
The sight of Rika swaying and making those strange sounds was both grotesque and frightening, yet at the same time somehow comical.
...She surely never imagined the drug she'd meant for her opponent would end up in her own veins.@k Poetic justice.@k How utterly pathetic.
"Aha ha ha ha ha!@k@vS06/06/150602070. Truly comical—this is hilarious.@k@vS06/06/150602071. Is the room spinning, or can't you breathe?"
changeReason: Old invents dialogue ('please don't throw up here, go to the bathroom'); new correctly translates 目が回ってるのかな、それとも息ができないのかな as 'Is the room spinning? Or can you not breathe?'--entirely different content.

TextENGNew: ``Heh heh heh!@k@vS06/06/150602073. Watching how long it takes you to drop dead should be quite entertaining.``
...Ah, but that would rob me of the pleasure of 'interrogation,' wouldn't it...!!

``You know, I'd been planning to use the nail table on you this whole time.@k@vS06/06/150602076. Whether you die from your own drug first or from having nails hammered into all ten fingers until the pain kills you--I'd love to find out.``

``...All right, it's decided. Let's do that.@k@vS06/06/150602078. Shall we relocate? To the fun, fun Underground Ritual Storehouse.@k@vS06/06/150602079. I'll need you to take a little nap so I can move you, okay?@k@vS06/06/150602080. Heh heh heh!!``
Crackling my stun gun menacingly, I approached, wearing a look of malice and defiance to rival the one Rika herself had shown.
The instant Rika saw the blue-white sparks from the stun gun,@k she moved as if electrified, snatching up a thick, menacing kitchen knife that lay on the cutting board by the sink.

``...Oh? Still got that much fight in you?@k@vS06/06/150602082. Heh heh heh!@k@vS06/06/150602083. That's more like it--wouldn't be fun otherwise.@k@vS06/06/150602084. I've got plenty of questions for you, after all.``
``You'd be better off not resisting pointlessly, you know?@k@vS06/06/150602086. Then again, you'll suffer either way, heh heh heh!``
``...Sorry, but I'll have to decline your invitation, you cold-blooded wretch.``
changeReason: Old translates 冷血女 (cold-blooded woman) as 'torture maniac'; new correctly renders it as 'cold-blooded wretch.'

TextENGNew: The words that spilled from this girl at death's door were nothing I could have imagined from her usual demeanor.
But I didn't flinch.
My absolute advantage was unshakable, so there was nothing to rush.
Besides, she was still the last survivor of the Furude family, one of the Three Families.
Her transformation was, for some reason... oddly natural, and strangely devoid of any sense of incongruity.

``Compared to the blood-soaked history of your Three Families, I'm nothing at all.@k@vS06/06/150602088. To receive such a compliment from the esteemed Furude family head...@k@vS06/06/150602089. I'm rather honored...``
``So?@k@vS06/06/150602091. Are you going to put up a fight with a single knife in that wobbling body of yours?@k@vS06/06/150602092. You can barely even stand...``

``...You're right.@k@vS06/05/150500062. Like you say, this seems to be my limit.@k@vS06/05/150500063. ...Rather than be captured and killed by the likes of you, I'm afraid I'll be taking my exit first.``
``Your exit?@k@vS06/06/150602094. Pff--go ahead and try!!``
The absolute advantage was already clear.
And yet, we were both enveloped in a strange, fevered exhilaration.
'Lost our minds' wasn't quite the right expression for people of Hinamizawa.
...It was more like the liberation that comes when modern-day demons no longer need to pretend to be human.
Yes, it was... a mad banquet of blood-drinking demons.
The head of the Furude family and the head of the Sonozaki family--the old houses whose blood ran thickest with the demons'--locked in mutual frenzy.
I positioned myself with the hallway at my back, blocking her retreat.
The windows had iron grates and the side door had a chain. If she foolishly turned her back to me, I could hit her with the stun gun that instant.
Rika Furude's only 'exit' would have to go through me and into the hallway beyond...!
Rika brought her knife up smoothly into a stance.
...I read her as certain to thrust at my midsection, and braced myself sideways, crackling my stun gun...
Then Rika suddenly turned her back to me.
I'd expected her to rush at me, so I held my position and watched her suspicious movements carefully.
Rika, still gripping the knife, pressed its handle against the wall, standing the blade upright.@k ...I couldn't comprehend what she was doing.
``...Well then, goodbye, you cold-blooded wretch.@k@vS06/05/150500065. I refuse to be killed by the likes of you.``
Rika reared her head far back, then slammed her throat into the knife blade she'd braced against the wall...!!
Fine droplets of vivid blood scattered across the surroundings.
Rika reared back again and again... driving her throat against the blade's edge.@k Again... and again.
Her eyes were wide open, her expression unearthly.

...It was a face that simultaneously possessed the organic quality of the living and the inorganic quality of the dead and the doll,@k ...indescribably terrifying and eerie... a face drained utterly white.
But that otherworldly visage... was a mask perfectly befitting us demons.
Without a mirror, only Rika's face appeared that way.@k Had I been able to see my own, I was certain it would have been identical.
Each time Rika struck her throat, exquisitely fine crimson decorated the dull white surroundings in vivid splashes.
Yes, it was a dance.
...A dedicatory dance performed by the last head of the Furude family.
The demons' final dance of offering to Oyashiro-sama.
Before that literally blood-curdling dance, I roared ferociously.
It had no meaning--a primal, animal howl.
It was the same howl two beasts would make, bloodied and tearing at each other's throats,@k the survivor throwing back its head to bellow while watching the other convulse in a pool of blood.
My entire body trembled violently, erupting with the euphoria of standing before death--the most impartial verdict, more absolute than any judge's ruling.
The demon's howl.@k Screaming.@k A war cry.
It gradually faded, the demon's voice giving way to my own human voice.
...As a demon, I howled. As a human, I sneered.
From Rika's neck down to her feet, to her very footprints, everything was painted in a single shade of vivid red.
And still, Rika looked into my eyes.
I looked into hers.
...Whether what I was looking at could still be called eyes was doubtful.
...They were no longer human eyes... but the impossible gaze of the dead.
Rika... no... the corpse that had been Rika... seemed to smile as blood poured from her mouth.
Then she dropped the knife.
Then... she thrust both hands forward, fingers clawing, and they crawled toward her own throat.
It was the strangest thing I'd ever seen in my life.
I had never witnessed such a movement before.
...I think Rika smiled.
...Had she been a living person, I might have called that expression a smile.
...Then, as her eyes suddenly snapped wide open......
``...Wh... WHAAAAAHHHHHHHH!!!``

Bathed in my scream,@k ...Rika's expression finally crossed from the boundary between life and death@k to that of the truly dead... and she collapsed face-first into her own pool of blood with a wet splash.
That was the end of it.
I stumbled backward from the body of Rika Furude, my back hitting the hallway wall...
RAAAAAAAAHHHHHHHH!!
I howled again... in tribute to the last head of the Furude family and her death-throes dedicatory dance.
Euphoria surged through my entire body. My blood felt like it was boiling, my veins writhing and dancing throughout my body until I felt almost ticklish.
``I won... I won...!!!@k@vS06/06/150602099. I won, I prevailed!!@k@vS06/06/150602100. RAAAAAAHHHH!!!``
From that primitive joy of having survived a fight to the death, I came to realize something: I had now eliminated the heads of all Three Families--the very core of Oyashiro-sama's Curse.
Yes--now everything was laid bare.
Oyashiro-sama's Curse--its mechanisms, its masterminds--all of it was exposed.
The embodiments of the curse that had swallowed Satoshi-kun into his fate of death--the heads of the Three Families--had all fallen into my hands.
The Sonozaki family head had died of shock.
The Kimiyoshi family head was captured.
The Furude family head had fallen to her own poison fang,@k dying a death befitting the last of the Three Families of Onigafuchi.
Yes--I had done it.@k I had reached the end.
The ringleaders, the masterminds who had drawn Satoshi-kun into the curse!@k I had destroyed them all.@k Every last one!!
Satoshi-kun had been so powerless.@k He had no strength to defy his fate.
They branded that gentle, fragile Satoshi-kun as a member of the cursed Houjou family@k and threw him into the vortex of death, sneering as he was swallowed up!!
Unforgivable, unforgivable, unforgivable...!!!
He was a Houjou, so being cursed to death was only natural, dying was only natural, perfectly expected.
Under the name of Oyashiro-sama's Curse, it didn't matter when or how they killed him!!
I will never forgive any of those who built such a world!!!
How could I have saved Satoshi-kun?
I couldn't save him.
Shion Sonozaki couldn't have done any more than she did...!!
If only I had been Mion, not Shion!!
If only I had still been Mion even today!!!
I could have saved him--if I had been Mion, I could have saved Satoshi-kun!!
That's right--it was her fault!!@k Shion!!@k She had to go and say she wanted to try that sea bream sashimi!!@k I was the one who was supposed to eat it!!
But Shion whined and cried about it being so unfair...@k and I, feeling generous like a big sister, thought I'd indulge my little sister's selfishness!!!@k It was only supposed to be for one night!!!
Mion always got the good treatment... so I felt sorry for Shion!@k That was all there was to it!!!@k I was being a good big sister, just granting my little sister's wish!!
When dawn broke.
The world had been turned upside down, and it stayed that way until this very day.
We were no longer the same twins.
The one with the demon tattoo was Mion; the one without was Shion.@k What was that supposed to mean...?!
Wait, wait... Mom, listen, Mom, listen!!@k I'm Mion, I'm Mion!!!
You can tell us apart, can't you, Mom?!@k Then look--you can see I'm Mion, right?!@k Tell everyone I'm Mion...!!
I'm Mion, I'm Mion!!@k Don't call me Shion!!@k I'm Mion, I'm Mioooon!!!
``That's when I understood clearly.@k@vS06/06/150602102. ...You knew, didn't you.@k@vS06/06/150602103. That night wasn't just a party when the relatives gathered--you knew what it really was.``
``N-No, I didn't!!@k@vS06/03/150300406. I really, truly didn't know!!``
``Hah?! Don't give me that!!@k@vS06/06/150602105. You were unusually persistent that day, weren't you?!!``

``You said you'd never had sea bream sashimi, you wanted to try it, Mion always gets the good stuff, you wanted a turn too!!@k@vS06/06/150602107. You're usually so docile about it, but that one day you were relentless!!!``
``I didn't know--I truly didn't know!!@k@vS06/03/150300408. If I'd known something that horrible would happen... I would never have said it...!!``
``Oh, sure you wouldn't!!``
"You were always jealous, weren't you—always saying it wasn't fair that Mion got all the good treatment!!"
changeReason: Old invents 'Always looking at me with those foxy eyes'; new correctly translates 魅音ばかりいい目にあってずるい as 'always saying Mion gets all the good treatment, it's not fair.'

TextENGNew: ``That's why I was nice enough to switch with you once in a while!!``
``But you weren't satisfied with that. You grew even more jealous.``
``And that night, you alone knew what it meant, and you stole it from me.``
``You stole Mion from me!!!``
``Give her back!!``
``I'm the one who's Mion.``
``I am Mion Sonozaki!!``
``You're Shion--you've always been Shion!!``
``Don't you dare wear my name, you impostor!!!``
Roaring, I kicked the iron bars of the cell again and again and again!!
The violent metallic clanging of each kick was nothing compared to my howl--like a frightened puppy's whimper.
That's right--I had been trapped by something truly absurd.
It wasn't that I was Shion because I lacked the demon tattoo. That was merely a symbol others used to distinguish us sisters.
changeReason: Old omits the first clause entirely ('It wasn't that I was Shion because I lacked the demon tattoo'); new preserves this key identity-theme statement.

TextENGNew: Even without the demon mark, I was Mion. I was allowed to be Mion. I AM Mion Sonozaki...!!!
I swept my hair up and bound it with the yellow rubber band from my pocket--the one used for Mion's hairstyle.
The feeling of hair against the nape of my neck vanished,@k and with it, my thoughts seemed to clear--not just my neck, but my entire mind.
Through the iron bars... two Mions faced each other.
Under the rules we sisters shared, this was something that should absolutely never happen.
Yes--there was only one Mion and one Shion!
The two could never exist simultaneously!

``Let your hair down, Shion!!!@k@vS06/06/150602120. I am Mion--Mion is right here!@k@vS06/06/150602121. You were never Mion!!@k@vS06/06/150602122. So don't you ever impersonate me again--do you hear me, Shion?!!``
``Do you hear me?! If you can hear me, answer! Make your answer reach me, SHIIOOOONNN!!!``
On that day.@k I reclaimed my true self, and Shion acknowledged that she had always been Shion.

``Haaah... how wonderful it is to be Mion!@k@vS06/06/150602125. Everything responds perfectly, my whole body is mine,@k@vS06/06/150602126. my mind feels sharper,@k@vS06/06/150602127. of course--Mion is capable of anything,@k@vS06/06/150602128. unlike the worthless Shion.``

``How dare you clumsily play at being Mion when you're nothing but a failure--do you have any idea how much that made my skin crawl?!@k@vS06/06/150602130. You of all people should understand!!@k@vS06/06/150602131. Shion?!``
Shion spoke the word acknowledging me as her elder sister.
Yes--I was Mion!!!@k RAAAAAHHHH!!!
The demon sleeping at the very bottom of the true Mion Sonozaki was finally permitted to awaken.
The demon shook with the ecstasy of liberation, roaring as it sent tremors through my entire body.

``Shion, you have committed a great sin.@k@vS06/06/150602133. Until now, you were Mion.@k@vS06/06/150602134. You held the powerful position of Mion, heir to the Sonozaki family's demon, yet you never tried to save Satoshi-kun--you never even attempted it!``
``You had ten billion moments in which you could have saved him, and every single time, you chose to let him die.``
changeReason: Old renders 百億 (ten billion) as 'millions'; new correctly says 'ten billion moments,' preserving the deliberate hyperbolic scale of the JPN.

TextENGNew: ``I will never forgive you--never! If I had been you, I could have saved Satoshi-kun!!@k@vS06/06/150602137. I will never forgive you for stealing me away and killing him!!!``
Shion cowered and trembled beyond the iron bars, shrinking from the hatred blazing in my eyes...

``I will kill you--I will absolutely kill you!!@k@vS06/06/150602142. After I've killed you, I'll throw you into the same well as Satoshi-kun.@k@vS06/06/150602143. Go apologize to him in the next world!!!``
``That is the price for impersonating me all this time!!@k@vS06/06/150602145. Retribution!!@k@vS06/06/150602146. Your sin!!@k@vS06/06/150602147. AAAAAHHHHHH!!!``
I needed to at least clean the kitchen enough to be usable.@k While I was spreading old newspapers across the kitchen floor to soak up the blood, the phone rang.
...I didn't feel like answering, but it might cause trouble if they thought no one was home.
Better to pick up and respond properly.
``Hello, Sonozaki residence.``
``Thank you for your time. This is the Houjou residence.``
I was startled for an instant by someone giving the Houjou name on the phone,@k but immediately realized it was that detestable Satoko.
``I'm terribly sorry for calling so late...@k@vS06/04/150400025. Is our Rika not there with you, by any chance?``
Of course.@k Since being left alone, Satoko Houjou had been living with Rika Furude.
It was only natural she'd grow suspicious when Rika didn't come home.
``Yeah, she's here.``
``Oh my, she's still there?!@k@vS06/04/150400027. It's well past dinnertime!``

``You should come over too, Satoko.@k@vS06/06/150602158. I've got plenty for you as well.@k@vS06/06/150602159. Rika-chan's already eating.``
